Interface Flammable

All Known Implementing Classes:
BaseUnit, LeaderUnit, Tile
public interface Flammable
A contract for game entities or environmental elements (such as tiles or units) that can interact with fire mechanics. Implementing classes must define how the object catches fire, behaves while burning, and is extinguished.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    burn()
    Executes the recurring logic that happens while the object is on fire.
    void
    extinguish()
    Forces the fire on the object to go out, immediately ending any active burning states or ongoing fire effects.
    void
    ignite()
    Triggers the ignition process, setting the object on fire if it is flammable.
    boolean
    isBurning()
    Checks if the object is currently actively on fire.
    boolean
    isFlammable()
    Checks if the object is susceptible to catching fire.

  • Method Details

    • isBurning

      boolean isBurning()
      Checks if the object is currently actively on fire.
      Returns:
      true if the object is currently burning; false otherwise.

    • isFlammable

      boolean isFlammable()
      Checks if the object is susceptible to catching fire. This may depend on its inherent properties (e.g., a forest is flammable, water is not) or its current state (e.g., it might not be flammable if it has already burnt to ash).
      Returns:
      true if the object can be ignited; false otherwise.

    • ignite

      void ignite()
      Triggers the ignition process, setting the object on fire if it is flammable. This typically changes its state so that isBurning() returns true and sets up any necessary burning durations or effects.

    • burn

      void burn()
      Executes the recurring logic that happens while the object is on fire. This method is usually called once per turn or tick and handles effects such as taking burn damage, spreading fire, or reducing the remaining burn duration.

    • extinguish

      void extinguish()
      Forces the fire on the object to go out, immediately ending any active burning states or ongoing fire effects.
